badges.label: The Grinder
badges.flavor: Can't miss a game. Every slate, every week.
badges.description: Placed the most picks this season.

badges.label: The Choker
badges.flavor: Went all in… and all in went wrong.
badges.description: Worst win rate on All-In picks this season.

badges.label: The Whale
badges.flavor: Goes big and cashes — the house pays this one.
badges.description: Best win rate on All-In picks this season (minimum number of All-Ins required).

badges.label: The Ghost
badges.flavor: Showed up for the group chat. Not the picks.
badges.description: Missed the most picks this season.

badges.label: The Nemesis
badges.flavor: Nobody wants to be on the other side of this matchup.
badges.description: Best head-to-head record this season on games where you picked the opposite side.

badges.label: The Homer
badges.flavor: Picks on vibes and team colors.
badges.description: Biggest share of picks on a single team (minimum number of picks required).

badges.label: Big Game Hunter
badges.description: Won 3 or more All-In picks this season.

badges.label: Perfect Week
badges.flavor: Not a single wrong pick all week.
badges.description: Had at least one week with every pick correct and none missed.

badges.label: Lone Wolf
badges.flavor: Runs with no one. Fades the whole flock.
badges.description: Fades the group's consensus more often than anyone this season (minimum picks required).

badges.label: The Sheep
badges.flavor: Goes where the group goes. Safety in numbers.
badges.description: Most often picks with the group's consensus (minimum picks required).

badges.label: The Oracle
badges.flavor: Bucks the crowd and wins. Madness or genius?
badges.description: Best win rate on picks made against the majority this season (minimum picks required).

badges.label: The Lemming
badges.flavor: Followed the herd. Right off the cliff.
badges.description: Worst win rate on picks made with the majority this season (minimum picks required).

badges.label: Chalk Eater
badges.flavor: Never met a favorite they didn't back.
badges.description: Biggest share of picks on the spread favorite (minimum picks required).

badges.label: Dog Lover
badges.flavor: Loyalty over logic. Always takes the longshot.
badges.description: Biggest share of picks on the spread underdog (minimum picks required).

badges.label: Hot Hand
badges.flavor: Can't miss right now. Every pick is money.
badges.description: Longest correct-pick streak this season (minimum picks required). Provisional in-season; crowned at season end on the longest run achieved.

badges.label: The Comeback
badges.flavor: Started in the basement. Finished somewhere else entirely.
badges.description: Climbed further from their season low point to their final rank than anyone else.

badges.label: Best of the Rest
badges.flavor: Buried in the standings. Unbeatable for a week.
badges.description: Posted a week's single highest score at least once while sitting in the bottom half of the standings.

player.prose: You finished No. 1 with 105 points and a 36-17-2 record, and once you grabbed the throne you never looked back. Week 5 was pure arson — 18 points and a Perfect Week — and that 8-1-1 All-In mark is the kind of heater that makes the rest of Sunday Bets look like they were donating 💰. Even your wobble in Week 2 at -6 just set up The Comeback, because champs don’t stay down; they bury the league, cash big spots, and call it a season.

league.prose: Three lead changes, a 6-point title margin, and in the end Beth snatched the crown at 105 while Hank’s 13-game heater still only bought him second at 99 — hell of a run, no parade. Doug deserves a special slow clap for turning a season that once had him at #1 into a faceplant all the way down to 4th, finishing with 74 while Charlie slid right past him to 75 by a single damn point. And then there’s Mike: dead last at -144, proudly hauling home The Choker, The Lemming, and Chalk Eater like a one-man disaster class. Beth held #1 for 10 weeks and closed the job; everybody else was just background noise 👑

recaps.prose: Beth slams the regular season door shut at 105 points, holding Hank off by 6 like a proper champion while stretching the hottest hand in the room to 3 straight wins 👑. Charlie made the loudest mess of Week 14: week-best 8 points, a gutsy 33.33% contrarian hit, and a last-day shove past Doug for 3rd, 75-74 — Doug tried to fade the crowd and got cooked for it. And then there’s Mike, who somehow found a trapdoor beneath last place: week-worst -19, a blown All-In, and a season-ending crater at -144. Marcus at least nailed the All-In hero bit, but even that couldn’t hide a heavy loss and a brutal -32 finish.

Charlie kicked the door off the hinges in Week 13: perfect week, 19 points, and he cashed the All-In too — the kind of heater that makes third place at 75 suddenly feel a lot louder. Meanwhile Mike put on a disasterclass, faceplanting to -23 and torching his All-In for good measure; at -144 on the season, that’s not a slump, that’s a crater. Up top, Beth’s still wearing the crown at 105 with a 3-win streak, but with Hank lurking at 99 the title race is only 4 points wide, so she can’t afford many more cute fades that backfire. And Doug? Congrats on the scenic route from #2 to #3 — got cooked by a week where Charlie remembered how to collect.

Hank kicked the damn door in this week: a flawless card, 19 points, hit the All-In, and even nailed the 33.33% contrarian like he was playing against children. That heater vaulted him from 3rd to 2nd, and now Beth’s still on the throne at 105 — but that 3-win streak is the only thing keeping her 10 points clear, because Hank is at 99 and coming loud. Meanwhile Charlie lit his All-In on fire and still sits on 75, while Mike somehow found a way to post a hideous -17 week and drag his season corpse to -144 💀.

Mike dropped a 14-point nuke on the week and still sits at -144 on the season, which is the kind of “too little, way too damn late” heater this league deserves. The real bloodletting was Charlie: -2 on the week, dumped from 3rd to 4th by Hank, while Hank stayed perfect and tightened that head-to-head to 54-51 all-time — your lead in that rivalry isn’t looking so comfy now. Up top, Beth keeps the crown at 105 with a 3-win streak and the All-In cash, but let’s not pretend she walked on water: even the leader managed a backfired fade, same as Charlie and Doug, and the top of this thing is still close enough for somebody to get cooked.

Marcus didn’t just finish last — he faceplanted into -11 and lit his All-In ticket on fire, while Mike somehow keeps digging and now sits at a grotesque -144 on the season. Hank took the weekly crown with 9, but Beth is still the one hogging the throne: 105 points, a 3-win heater, and a 7-point cushion over second while the rest of you keep pretending this is a race. Doug gets half a clap for cashing a 40% contrarian hit, then loses the other half for a backfired fade — classic “look at me, I’m sharp” stuff that still leaves him fourth at 74. And Hank, before you puff your chest too hard, Beth still owns you head-to-head 57-49 👑
